behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler↔signature-verifier↔queue handoff: reject path must both set HTTP 401 and suppress enqueue call. MOCK-MISS: this is cross-module handoff/state, not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required; in-process request/response harness and fake queue are sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher level: behavior text only requires '401' and 'not forwarded', not real network timing/infra effects.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real webhook handler with real signature-check module and a spy/fake queue writer; send a request with mismatched body/signature and assert status=401 and zero queue writes.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering guarantee across modules (HTTP handler and persistent queue adapter). MOCK-MISS: a unit test of either side alone misses ordering race. REAL-INFRA: can be validated with in-process app wiring plus local persistent queue backend/test double that records commit-before-response ordering; no real external network/subprocess required. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required OS/network/subprocess boundary, (b) no AI output dependency, (c) no multi-agent chain. Rejected System: behavior text asks ordering 'written ... before ... 200', not real HTTP transport behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler + queue module together; instrument queue 'persist success' event and response send time, assert persist completes before 200 is emitted.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Failure occurs at orchestration boundary: enqueue/dispatch should decouple from downstream worker completion. MOCK-MISS: single-function tests miss wait-on-worker coupling bugs. REAL-INFRA: in-process modules with intentionally slow downstream worker are enough; no real network/subprocess required. Rejected System: behavior text is about not waiting for downstream completion, not transport/OS behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler with real dispatch path and a downstream processor that blocks; assert response is returned before processor completion signal.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on wall-clock latency under concurrency, which is sensitive to runtime scheduling, event-loop contention, socket handling, and real HTTP timing. MOCK-HIDE: in-process/fake-time tests can pass while real server under concurrent load violates SLA. REAL-INFRA => System by definition. Rejected Integration: wording 'within 3 seconds wall-clock' and 'slow and concurrent' requires real timing behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Start the service process, send concurrent real HTTP requests while earlier events trigger slow downstream work, and measure end-to-end response latency; assert each response <=3s.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness is at module/data boundary (handler + idempotency store/queue write path). MOCK-MISS: pure unit logic misses duplicate race/state propagation between components. REAL-INFRA: can be caught with integrated local persistence layer; no required external network/subprocess. Rejected System: behavior text targets duplicate write prevention, not OS/network-specific effects.
behaviors.test_description: Send two requests with same event ID through the wired handler and persistent queue stack; assert first write succeeds and second produces no additional queue record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: This validates contract mapping across queue module and HTTP layer (queue-full signal -> 503 + header). MOCK-MISS: unit-testing response formatting alone misses integration of queue state propagation. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess behavior, (b) no AI dependency, (c) no agentic sequence. Rejected System: behavior text is status/header translation from queue condition, not real network fault semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Integrate handler with queue module configured to return 'full'; assert HTTP 503 and presence/value format of Retry-After header.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is queue logic + persistence layer interaction enforcing configured capacity across writes. Not just happy-path function math; requires module wiring with real/local storage semantics. REAL-INFRA: local DB/filesystem-backed queue fits Integration per level defs; no external HTTP/subprocess required. Rejected System: behavior text says 'persistent queue ... enforces configured capacity' but does not require OS/network phenomena beyond local component integration.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue against local test storage with a small configured limit, enqueue up to limit, then assert next enqueue is rejected with capacity error and no extra record persisted.
